name: level
definition: Loudness above the run's own speed-conditioned smooth-road floor (delta-dB), per band.
indicators: delta-dB: 10*log10(window band energy / per-speed-conditioned floor energy)

detection.measure: event median level_db minus floorAt(band, speed), normalised by LEVEL_NORM_DB=20 into [0,1]

confidence.basis: delta-dB magnitude relative to the per-run noise floor; SNR of the window

discrimination_test.claim: typical smooth-road delta-dB vs worst rough-chop delta-dB
discrimination_test.result: median 2.2-2.7 dB (flat across 0-20 m/s) vs jc4 worst-chop 7-13 dB (peak 13.1 dB)

notes: No idle-vs-driving delta-dB pair is recorded in the research (delta-dB is defined relative to each run's own per-speed floor, so an idle stop is close to 0 by construction - that is what 'stop goes quiet' means for tonality, not for level). The discrimination_test above uses the closest real figures: docs/2026-07-04-cross-pass-rough-spot-aggregation-findings.md's typical-vs-worst-chop delta-dB spread, which is the actual empirical evidence for this tag's loudness dimension.
